Hero.desc: Full stack developer with a soft spot for front-end, philosophy, and Raspberry Pi.

Featured.title: Featured
Featured.paragraph1: I've developed a mobile app for tracking and rating readings. Each user can search for or scan a book, rate it, leave a comment, manage their reading status, and much more !
Featured.paragraph2: The unique feature of this app is that your profile page reflects your reading habits. You can create different bookshelves, add as many books as you want, and add a description.
Featured.paragraph3: You can arrange the bookshelves however you like on your profile, which also displays your reading goal and what you're currently reading. All the information you need to let your friends know your current reading mood !

my-shelf.description: Site vitrine pour la boite de production dependant
longDescription: Site vitrine réalisé pour un client dans le secteur de l'audiovisuel.
Focus sur la mise en avant du contenu produits, type vidéos et photos. Optimisation des requêtes, SEO et gestion vidéos.
Utilisation de Strapi couplé à un VPS pour la gestion du changement de contenu de manière simple par le client.

Using Strapi coupled with a VPS for easy content change management by the client.

About.title: About me
About.paragraph1: I started coding out of curiosity, to understand how the websites I visited were made. Very quickly, the desire to build took precedence over the desire to consume, and it has never left me since.
About.paragraph2: The front end is my playground, but I'm involved in the entire chain: back end, data, infrastructure.
About.paragraph3: Outside of the screen, I read a lot. Philosophy, essays, fiction. That's what motivated me to create MyShelf, a tool for my reading, not a social network.
About.building: An air quality sensor for my bedroom.

Contact.desc: Have a project in mind, a question, or just want to discuss a book ? Write to me.
